deep_dive.title: From Risk Classification to Action Plan Remediation: A Guardrail Feedback Driven Framework for LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: LLM-based guardrails typically safeguard agents by evaluating proposed actions or inputs before execution, producing safety signals such as binary allow/deny decisions, risk categories, and/or explanatory rationales about potential policy violations. However, agent risks often arise when otherwise benign tasks are contaminated by untrusted external content, unsafe instructions, or risky tool use. Existing guardrails often flag the entire task uniformly as unsafe, thereby blocking the threat but sacrificing the benign part. Moreover, existing work largely evaluates guardrails in isolation, leaving unclear whether their interventions lead to safer downstream agent behavior. To address this, we introduce TRIAD (Tripartite Response for Iterative Agent Guardrailing), a guardrail-integrated agent framework that leverages guardrail-generated verbal feedback as a guiding signal to keep the agent aligned with benign objectives at each planning step. We finetune a language model on a self-curated training dataset to output one of three decisions: proceed, refuse, or update, together with structured natural-language feedback. Rather than merely allowing or blocking execution, update guides the agent to revise its plan, avoid harmful components, and preserve the benign task where possible. TRIAD injects this feedback into the agent's context, enabling subsequent plan revision and forming a closed loop between guardrail feedback and agent planning. Extensive experiments on ASB and AgentHarm show that TRIAD reduces the average attack success rate to 10.42%, while achieving the best safety-utility trade-off among guardrail-integrated baselines. Our code is available at: https://github.com/YUHAOSUNABC/TRIAD.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: guardrail feedback framework for LLM agents with risk classification and remediation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向LLM智能体的护栏反馈框架，包含风险分类与修复
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向LLM智能体的护栏反馈框架，包含风险分类与修复
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向LLM智能体的护栏反馈框架，包含风险分类与修复
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces TRIAD, a guardrail feedback-driven framework that classifies risks and generates action plans to remediate unsafe agent behavior, serving as an output guardrail for LLM agents.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM智能体护栏通常将整个任务标记为不安全，牺牲了良性部分，且缺乏对下游行为影响的评估。本文提出TRIAD（三方护栏），该框架首先对输入/动作进行风险评估，然后生成修复行动计划，在阻止威胁的同时保留合法操作。在多个智能体任务上，TRIAD相比基线在安全性和可用性上取得更好平衡。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM智能体护栏通常将整个任务标记为不安全，牺牲了良性部分，且缺乏对下游行为影响的评估。本文提出TRIAD（三方护栏），该框架首先对输入/动作进行风险评估，然后生成修复行动计划，在阻止威胁的同时保留合法操作。在多个智能体任务上，TRIAD相比基线在安全性和可用性上取得更好平衡。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: output guardrails for large language models

deep_dive.title: Towards Responsibly Non-Compliant Machines
deep_dive.abstract: We consider the problem of engineering autonomous intelligent agents that are capable to responsibly not comply with user requests. We argue that machine non-compliance comes in many different forms, and sketch the issues we should pursue on the road of accomplishing responsibly non-compliant intelligent machines. We anchor responsible non-compliance in justifications for task refusal, pathways to override the non-compliance, as well as careful tracking of security risks and liability transfers.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: responsible non-compliance framework for autonomous agent safety
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自主智能体负责任不服从的安全框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自主智能体负责任不服从的安全框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自主智能体负责任不服从的安全框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes responsible non-compliance for autonomous agents, including justifications for refusal, overrides, and security tracking, directly addressing agent safety.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文探讨如何设计能够负责任地拒绝用户请求的自主智能体，论证了机器不服从的多种形式，并提出任务拒绝的合理性、覆盖机制以及安全风险和责任的追踪路径，为智能体安全提供了重要理论框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文探讨如何设计能够负责任地拒绝用户请求的自主智能体，论证了机器不服从的多种形式，并提出任务拒绝的合理性、覆盖机制以及安全风险和责任的追踪路径，为智能体安全提供了重要理论框架。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: safety mechanisms for autonomous agents based on LLMs
